eventMetadata.context_description: Recent US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, on April 11-12—led by US Vice President JD Vance, Jared Kushner, and Steve Witkoff alongside Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi—collapsed after 21 hours without agreement, despite reports of some progress on ceasefire terms amid the ongoing conflict. The failure prompted the US to initiate a naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz starting April 14, escalating economic pressures as the fragile ceasefire expires April 21. Pakistan has proposed follow-up negotiations, while traders weigh Tehran's uranium enrichment stance against Washington's demands for verifiable limits, with diplomatic breakthroughs hinging on de-escalation signals before potential military intensification.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA's commanding $4.6 trillion market capitalization as of mid-April 2026—over $800 billion ahead of Alphabet and Apple—underpins trader consensus at 98.8% implied probability for it remaining the world's largest company by April 30. This positioning stems from unrelenting demand for its GPUs in artificial intelligence training and inference, bolstered by successful Blackwell platform ramps and data center revenue surges reported in recent quarters, widening the gap versus consumer-focused rivals like Apple facing iPhone sales softness. No major developments in the past week indicate challengers closing in, with stable semiconductor supply chains reinforcing NVIDIA's lead. Realistic upset scenarios include an explosive rally in Alphabet's cloud AI or Microsoft's Azure growth, or unforeseen regulatory scrutiny on NVIDIA's export controls, though these appear low-probability given two weeks to resolution.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar's center-right Tisza party captured a two-thirds supermajority in Hungary's April 12, 2026, parliamentary election, decisively defeating Viktor Orbán's Fidesz and prompting the incumbent prime minister's immediate concession after 16 years in power. Official results confirm Tisza's control of the 199-seat National Assembly, enabling rapid government formation and Magyar's confirmation without coalition negotiations. This outcome reflects voter rejection of Orbán-era policies amid economic pressures and corruption allegations, aligning trader consensus at 98.8% for Magyar. While legal challenges to results or presidential delays could theoretically intervene, the landslide margin and procedural timelines make such shifts improbable before resolution.

title: Counter-Strike: RED Canids vs Gentle Mates (BO3) - IEM Rio Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket quarterfinal 1 match between RED Canids and Gentle Mates in the IEM Rio Group A, initially scheduled for April 14 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "RED Canids" if RED Canids win the match against Gentle Mates.

This market will resolve to "Gentle Mates" if Gentle Mates win the match against RED Canids.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 1. 

If Map 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "RED Canids". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.groupItemTitle: Map Handicap: RED (-1.5) vs Gentle Mates (+1.5)

eventMetadata.context_description: In IEM Rio 2026 Group A double-elimination lower bracket round 1, RED Canids (#68 HLTV) host Gentle Mates (#38) in a high-stakes BO3 after both opener losses on April 13—RED Canids 0-2 to Vitality and Gentle Mates 1-2 to G2. Brazilian RED Canids draw home-crowd energy in Rio de Janeiro, bolstered by recent IGL reNTU addition (13 days ago) and 59.5% win rate last three months against regional foes, led by AWPer chayJESUS (1.08 rating). Stable Spanish Gentle Mates counter with 66.7% recent win rate and top performer MartinezSa (1.14 rating), but travel fatigue and losses to 9INE, NiP loom large. No head-to-head; map vetoes, roster synergy, and Overpass/Mirage form pivotal.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 78% implied probability to President Trump announcing the end of US military operations against Iran—initiated with airstrikes on February 28—by June 30, reflecting administration signals of nearing completion despite a fresh naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz that began April 13 after weekend peace talks collapsed in Islamabad. Late March statements indicated operations ahead of schedule, with Trump reportedly willing to conclude even if Tehran retains waterway control. A two-week ceasefire agreed April 7 expired without resolution, while House Democrats' war powers resolution was blocked April 9. Saudi pressure to lift the blockade and ongoing diplomacy could prompt an earlier announcement, though current escalation tempers near-term odds below 40% through April.
